mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate persistently signals weak aggregate demand and depressed corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, the average forward 20-day return when UNRATE is above its median is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production for two consecutive months signals a contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate output and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change is negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The trigger fires fewer than 5 times in a 10-year sample, or the median forward 60-day return following the trigger is not more negative than -2%.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment and retail sales decline simultaneously, it confirms a broad-based demand recession that sharply reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: Joint occurrence does not produce a median 20-day return worse than -1%, or signal predicts positive returns more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a post-recession recovery signal improving household confidence and construction activity, driving demand and earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During the defined regime, the average forward 20-day return when HOUST is rising is not > 0, or the one-sided t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer price inflation above 4% year-over-year triggers market expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: In episodes where the trigger fires, the median 5-day forward return is not negative, or negative returns occur less than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, making stocks more attractive relative to bonds and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is in the bottom third of its 5-year history, the average forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Combining a very low federal funds rate with falling industrial production indicates that monetary stimulus is failing to revive real output, signaling a liquidity trap where equity investors price in deflation risk and lower earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence does not predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate above 70%, or the signal produces average returns near zero.

mechanism_narrative: During an early expansionary phase characterized by very high unemployment, rising payroll employment signals that the labor market is healing and income generation is strengthening, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During regimes where UNRATE exceeds 8%, the average forward 20-day return following an increase in PAYEMS is not positive, or the correlation is not statistically significant.
